name: Cathe's Red Beans and Rice
recipeIngredient: 2 lbs Manda's sausage (spicy) or Jimmy Dean Mesquite link sausage, regular, sliced diagonally
recipeInstructions:
1. Empty the beans into a 6-quart saucepan. Run water over them with your hands, picking out any stones or bad beans. Drain and repeat until the water runs clear and has no sand.
2. Add enough water to cover the beans by about 4 inches. Place on a large burner, bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat slightly, boil gently for 3 minutes, then remove from burner and cover. Let covered pot sit, unopened, for an hour. Drain and reserve beans, discarding the juice.
3. Place ham pieces, 2 cups of water, chopped vegetables, and seasonings in a 12-quart saucepan or large Dutch oven. Stir well, cover, and bring to boil over high heat. Reduce heat and simmer until meat is fork tender, about an hour, stirring occasionally.
4. Remove the meat from pan and tear into small pieces. Set aside. (If using ham hocks, cover with water to cook. Beans will take longer to reach normal consistency; you may not need extra water.)
5. Add drained beans and 2 cups of water to pan with ham broth and vegetables. Bring to boil over medium heat, reduce heat, and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Add sliced sausage and 2 more cups of water if needed so the beans are covered in liquid. Continue simmering until beans start breaking into pieces, about 35 more minutes, stirring often.
7. If beans scorch, do not stir. Instead, immediately remove from heat and transfer to another pot without scraping scorched beans into mixture. Add ham pieces or hocks and cook for 10 more minutes, stirring often. Do not let the mixture dry out like refried beans but keep it thicker than cream soup.
8. To serve, mound 1/2 cup cooked rice in a bowl and cover with beans, ham, and sausage pieces. Serves 15 to 20 as side dish.
